snapshot.scope: Independent review of the PR package-impact classifier, fail-closed unknown handling, unconditional main-push macOS distribution evidence, and separation from full-gates.

reviewers.independence: Product Planning reviews the package-selection contract only; Development and Release Engineering still own classifier implementation, package jobs and artifacts.

dimensions.summary: The quality owner defines classification once and Release Engineering owns when package evidence runs.
Both owners require main packaging, impact-selected PR packaging and fail-closed unknown impact.
Requirement, Exit, Phase and semantic claims remain unchanged by this CI scheduling contract.
Pure governance and fixture work avoids package cost while runtime, packaging and security-sensitive changes retain it.
Two existing owners carry the rule without a dashboard, new document or duplicated path catalog.

verification.evidence: The two current owners remain mapped, linked and contract-valid.
No new durable product decision or status projection is required.
The bounded replacement remains within all documentation budgets.

coordination.compatibility: Full-gates remains the full test/governance owner; only the separate PR package decision changes, while main remains unconditional.
coordination.acknowledgement: Unknown impact packages fail closed; explicit release or security review can force package evidence.

nonBlockingRisks: Classifier implementation and workflow wiring remain Development-owned follow-up evidence.
Windows/Linux qualification remains deferred and unclaimed unless explicitly targeted.
